steps.title: Recognize equation types
steps.content: An equation has exactly one solution when simplification yields a unique value (conditional equation). It has no solution when it becomes a false statement (contradiction), and infinitely many when it becomes a true identity.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$\frac{1}{44}+x=x$': Subtract $$x$$: $$\frac{1}{44}=0$$. False — no solution. ✗
  • Choice B — '$\frac{1}{44}+x=-x-\frac{1}{44}$': Add $$x$$ to both sides: $$\frac{1}{44}+2x=-\frac{1}{44}$$. Then $$2x=-\frac{2}{44}$$, so $$x=-\frac{1}{44}$$. Exactly one solution. ✓
  • Choice C — '$\frac{1}{44}-x=\frac{1}{44}-x$': Both sides identical — true for all $$x$$. Infinitely many solutions. ✗
  • Choice D — '$\frac{1}{44}-x=-x$': Add $$x$$: $$\frac{1}{44}=0$$. False — no solution. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
